channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Roy Cooper, Michael Whatley set to compete for a high-stakes North Carolina U.S. Senate seat
item.description: Former Democratic North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper will face former Republican National Committee Chairman Michael Whatley in the state's toss-up U.S. Senate race.
item.content:encoded:
Former Democratic North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper will face former Republican National Committee Chairman Michael Whatley in the state's toss-up U.S. Senate race.

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: GOP primary for Texas Senate seat set for runoff; Democratic race too close too call
item.description: Incumbent Sen. John Cornyn will face Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in a May runoff. Vote counting was still underway in the Democratic primary between Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico.
item.content:encoded:
Incumbent Sen. John Cornyn will face Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in a May runoff. Vote counting was still underway in the Democratic primary between Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell/Getty Images; Tom Williams/CQ-Roll Call via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: Humans' pull toward alcohol may have ancient origins (according to chimp pee)
item.description: Scientists learned that wild African chimpanzees consume alcohol by eating fermented fruit, suggesting that human attraction to alcohol may have ancient evolutionary origins.
item.content:encoded: Scientists learned that wild African chimpanzees consume alcohol by eating fermented fruit, suggesting that human attraction to alcohol may have ancient evolutionary origins.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Pregnant migrant girls are being sent to a Texas shelter flagged as medically risky
item.description: Government officials and advocates for the children worry the goal is to concentrate them in Texas, where abortion is banned.
item.content:encoded:
Government officials and advocates for the children worry the goal is to concentrate them in Texas, where abortion is banned.

(Image credit: Patricia Lim)
item.dc:creator: Mark Betancourt

item.title: The 2026 World Cup faces big challenges with only 100 days to go
item.description: Will Iran compete? Will violence in Mexico flare up? And what about funding for host cities in the U.S.? With only 100 days left before it begins, the 2026 World Cup is facing a lot of uncertainty.
item.content:encoded:
Will Iran compete? Will violence in Mexico flare up? And what about funding for host cities in the U.S.? With only 100 days left before it begins, the 2026 World Cup is facing a lot of uncertainty.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: A glimpse of Iran, through the eyes of its artists and journalists
item.description: Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.content:encoded:
The Seed of the Sacred Fig, For The Sun After Long Nights, Cutting Through Rocks, It Was Just an Accident, Martyr!, and Kayhan Kalhor.'/>

Understanding one of the world's oldest civilizations can't be achieved through a single film or book. But recent works of literature, journalism, music and film by Iranians are a powerful starting point.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: President Trump is trying to make it harder to vote. Here's why that matters
item.description: Trump is promoting tighter restrictions on mail-in ballots as well as passage of the SAVE Act, which requires proof of citizenship to vote. UCLA professor Richard Hasen unpacks the ramifications.
item.content:encoded: Trump is promoting tighter restrictions on mail-in ballots as well as passage of the SAVE Act, which requires proof of citizenship to vote. UCLA professor Richard Hasen unpacks the ramifications.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: A new one-a-day-pill holds promise for HIV's 'forgotten population'
item.description: It's designed to take the place of complicated, multiple drug regimens that many people with HIV need to follow. And it's also beneficial because the HIV virus is always evolving.
item.content:encoded:
It's designed to take the place of complicated, multiple drug regimens that many people with HIV need to follow. And it's also beneficial because the HIV virus is always evolving.

(Image credit: Science Source)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: U.S. evacuates diplomats from Middle East. And, what to expect from N.C., Texas primaries
item.description: The U.S. has evacuated diplomats in the Middle East and closed several embassies as war in Iran intensifies. And, what to expect from the Senate races in the North Carolina and Texas primary elections.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. has evacuated diplomats in the Middle East and closed several embassies as war in Iran intensifies. And, what to expect from the Senate races in the North Carolina and Texas primary elections.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The candy heir vs. chocolate skimpflation
item.description: The grandson of the Reese's Peanut Butter Cups creator has launched a campaign against The Hershey Company, which owns the Reese's brand. He wants them to stop skimping on ingredients.
item.content:encoded: The grandson of the Reese's Peanut Butter Cups creator has launched a campaign against The Hershey Company, which owns the Reese's brand. He wants them to stop skimping on ingredients.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky
